name: U-Snap-Bac
description_short: Detroit-based nonprofit providing free housing counseling, credit education, and financial coaching to low-income residents since 1985.
description_long:
U-SNAP-BAC was founded in 1985 by Detroit residents committed to community improvement through coalition building and neighborhood development. The organization evolved from an 11-member consortium of community groups on Detroit's Eastside into a formalized nonprofit housing corporation in 1987. Their mission centers on preserving housing stock, preventing displacement, and strengthening neighborhoods through collaborative, community-controlled initiatives.

U-SNAP-BAC offers free counseling and coaching services including pre-purchase homebuyer education, credit counseling with soft credit pulls and debt-to-income analysis, rental housing assistance, eviction prevention programs, and personalized financial coaching. They also administer two government housing assistance programs: the Homeowners Property Exemption (HOPE) program for low-income property tax relief and the Senior Citizen Solid Waste Discount program for seniors 65+ with principal residence exemptions. All counseling services include one-on-one appointments with certified counselors, group workshops, and practical resources.

U-SNAP-BAC distinguishes itself through deep community roots, local ownership, and a holistic approach addressing root causes of housing instability rather than symptoms. Their pre-purchase counseling explicitly meets requirements for down payment assistance programs and qualifies borrowers for loan products requiring homebuyer education. The organization's structure—with a separate nonprofit housing corporation—demonstrates commitment to neighborhood preservation through direct intervention in housing development and repair.

As a free-help nonprofit, U-SNAP-BAC's primary limitation is geographic scope (Detroit-focused) and reliance on appointment scheduling and form submissions. They do not provide emergency financial assistance, direct lending, or credit dispute services. Their programs serve low- to moderate-income households, making them ideal for residents facing housing instability or seeking homeownership guidance, but not for those needing rapid cash access or sophisticated credit repair.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Free one-on-one credit counseling with soft credit pulls and debt-to-income ratio analysis included
Comprehensive pre-purchase homebuyer education meets requirements for down payment assistance programs and mortgage products
Eviction prevention program addresses root causes of housing instability through holistic support
Administers government housing programs (HOPE property tax exemption and Senior Solid Waste Discount) with no fees
Personalized financial coaching with group workshops and practical resources for building financial literacy
Long operating history (since 1985) with deep community roots and neighborhood-specific expertise
Rental counseling specifically helps navigate application processes and understand full housing costs
cons: Limited to Detroit area—services not available nationwide or for remote clients
No credit dispute or credit repair services; focused on education rather than direct credit report correction
Requires scheduling appointments and submitting forms—no immediate same-day assistance for emergencies
No direct financial assistance or lending products; counseling only guides users to other resources
Information on website does not specify counselor certifications (e.g., NFCC, HUD-approval status) beyond general experience claims
best_for: Detroit-area low-income homeowners seeking property tax relief or senior housing discounts
First-time homebuyers planning purchases and needing education to qualify for down payment assistance
Renters facing eviction or seeking guidance on affordable rental housing and application processes
Individuals with credit or financial challenges wanting free personalized coaching and debt-to-income planning
diagnosis: U-SNAP-BAC is best for Detroit-area residents with low-to-moderate incomes seeking free housing counseling, credit education, or homebuyer preparation. The primary caveat is geographic limitation (Detroit only) and that services are educational/advisory rather than providing emergency cash, credit disputes, or direct lending—they guide clients toward resources and sustainable financial habits rather than solving immediate crises.
services: Credit counseling with soft credit pull and debt-to-income ratio analysis
Rental housing counseling and application navigation
Eviction prevention program addressing financial and legal housing challenges
Personalized financial coaching with one-on-one sessions
Homeowners Property Exemption (HOPE) program administration for property tax reduction
Senior Citizen Solid Waste Discount program (for homeowners 65+ with principal residence exemption)
Practical financial resources and action plans tailored to homeownership sustainability
Community coalition building and neighborhood improvement initiatives
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
